title: 10 best herbal capsules for inflamed gums
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for inflamed gums are a natural remedy designed to soothe and alleviate gum inflammation. These capsules contain a blend of herbal extracts, such as turmeric, ginger, and clove oil, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the gums. By taking these capsules regularly, individuals can experience improved oral health, reduced bleeding and sensitivity, and enhanced confidence in their smile. For example, using products like Bio-Gum or Oralzyme can lead to significant improvements in gum health, allowing people to enjoy eating and socializing without worrying about painful gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower capsules helps with inflamed gums because they contain the anti-inflammatory compound echinacea, which has been shown to reduce swelling and pain in gum tissues. The capsules also contain flavonoids, which have antibacterial properties that help combat bacterial infections that can cause gum inflammation. Additionally, purple coneflower's antioxidant properties help soothe and calm irritated gums, promoting a healthy and comfortable oral environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Purple coneflower contains anti-inflammatory compounds like isoquercitrin and quercetin, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, alleviating discomfort and pain.

Antimicrobial properties: The herb's antimicrobial compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, help combat bacterial infections that can cause gum inflammation, promoting a healthy oral environment.
Antioxidant activity: Purple coneflower's antioxidant properties help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Iridoids, such as echinacoside and isobutylamides, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums, alleviating discomfort and pain.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, including chlorogenic acid and caffeic acid, exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that combat oxidative stress and inflammation in the gums, promoting healing and soothing irritated tissues.

Triterpenes: Triterpenes, such as echinacosides, possess immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties that help regulate the immune response, reduce inflammation, and promote the healing of inflamed gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain a high concentration of alkaloids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: They are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help soothe inflamed gums.
Leaves: They contain compounds like caffeic acid and chicoric acid, which have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea purpurea roots and flowers at peak potency in late summer or early fall season.
Dry the harvested roots and flowers using a food dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 8 hours.
Grind the dried roots and flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder at a high speed.
Mix 1 part dried echinacea powder with 2 parts cellulose and gelatin to create the capsule filling.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the echinacea mixture using a capsule filling machine at a rate of 200 capsules per hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Germander capsules helps with inflamed gums because they contain a concentrated extract of Teucrium chamaedrys, a natural herb that has anti-inflammatory properties. The capsules work to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with gum inflammation by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Additionally, germander's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial growth that can contribute to gum disease. By targeting both inflammation and infection, germander capsules provide a holistic approach to promoting healthy gums and preventing further damage.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Germander capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, alleviating discomfort and pain.

Antibacterial properties: The capsules' antimicrobial properties can combat bacteria that contribute to gum disease, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis, thereby preventing the progression of inflammation and promoting healing.
Antioxidant activity: Germander's antioxidant properties help protect gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate inflammation and promote chronic gum disease.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in inflamed gums.

Flavonoids: Teucrium chamaedrys contains various flavonoids, including kaempferol and quercetin, which possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to combat gum inflammation and infection.
Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can aid in reducing gum inflammation and preventing further infection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties which help soothe and heal inflamed gums.
Barks: The barks of Teucrium chamaedrys are used as they possess astringent and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce gum inflammation.
Stems: The stems are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and fight off bacterial infections that cause gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest teucrium chamaedrys leaves and flowers at peak potency when the plant is in full bloom in summer months.
Dry the harvested plant material using air circulation for at least 2 weeks to preserve its potency.
Crush the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for easy encapsulation.
Mix the plant powder with a natural capsule filler and a binding agent in a 1:1 ratio.
Fill gelatin capsules with the prepared mixture and seal them securely using a capsule filler machine.

The herb's antimicrobial compounds also combat bacterial infections that can contribute to gum inflammation. Additionally, lemon balm's soothing effects calm the mouth and gums, promoting a healthy balance in the oral microbiome. By addressing these underlying causes, herbal lemon balm capsules offer a natural and effective solution for alleviating inflamed gums.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Lemon balm's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can cause gum inflammation, reducing oxidative stress and promoting a healthy gum environment.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Compounds like rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid in lemon balm capsules have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with inflamed gums.

Calming effects on the mouth: Lemon balm's essential oils, such as citral and geraniol, have a calming effect on the mouth tissues, which can help reduce pain, anxiety, and stress associated with gum inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with inflamed gums.
Linalool: As a terpene, linalool has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections and prevent further inflammation in the gums.
Luteolin: This flavonoid has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help protect the gums from oxidative stress and promote healing in inflamed tissue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, which helps to soothe and calm inflamed gums.
Buds: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce swelling and prevent infection in inflamed gums.

Stems: They are used due to their rich content of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis flowers and leaves in the morning after the dew has dried for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer in a warm oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a high-speed electric herb grinder.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powdered plant material using a small spoon or capsule filler.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with inflamed gums because they contain potent anti-inflammatory properties derived from the flower's petals. The capsule's active ingredients, including flavonoids and saponins, have been shown to reduce swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation. By soothing and calming irritated tissues, pot marigold capsules can help alleviate symptoms of gingivitis, reducing redness, bleeding, and discomfort. This natural remedy promotes healthy gums by combating inflammation and promoting a balanced oral environment.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory: Pot Marigold's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the gums, soothing gum tissues and alleviating pain.

Wound Healing Properties: The herb's flavonoids and carotenoids promote wound healing by enhancing collagen synthesis, improving blood flow, and reducing bacterial adhesion to gum tissue, promoting faster recovery from gum inflammation.

Antibacterial Activity: Pot Marigold has been shown to exhibit antibacterial properties against various oral pathogens, including those that contribute to gum disease and inflammation, helping to prevent further infection and promote healthy gum tissue.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with inflamed gums.
Phenolic acids: They possess antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which help combat bacterial infections and minimize tissue damage caused by inflammation in the gums.
Nascent Oils (Carotenoids): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help soothe and calm inflamed tissues, promoting healing and reducing pain.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects.
Seeds: Have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as potential wound-healing effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 dried calendula officinalis flowers for the herbal ingredient.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the powdered calendula with 1 tablespoon of vegetable cellulose powder for encapsulation.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filling machine.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dark place for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage capsules helps with inflamed gums because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and pain. The bioactive compounds in sage, such as rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes that contribute to gum inflammation. Additionally, sage's antibacterial properties help eliminate harmful bacteria that can exacerbate periodontal disease, promoting a healthier mouth environment.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gums, promoting a healthy environment for gum tissue to thrive.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums, providing relief from discomfort and preventing further damage.

Antimicrobial properties: Sage's antimicrobial agents inhibit the growth of bacteria that can contribute to gum inflammation, such as Streptococcus mutans, helping to prevent infection and promote healing.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic Acid: A phenolic compound with potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in inflamed gums.
Carvacrol: A monoterpene with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to combat bacterial infections and reduce inflammation in the gums.
Salvianolic Acid: A caffeic acid derivative with strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in inflamed gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and heal inflamed gums.
Stems: They are used due to their antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and promote wound healing in the gums.
Roots: They are used due to their antibacterial properties, which help combat gum infections and promote overall oral health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried salvia officinalis leaves and flowers from a trusted supplier or your garden.
Clean the dried material by sifting it through a 100-micron sieve to remove any debris.
Use a coffee grinder to grind 50 grams of the cleaned salvia officinalis into a fine powder.
Mix 500 milliliters of distilled water with 5 grams of gelatin in a small saucepan over low heat.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the salvia officinalis powder and seal them immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with inflamed gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which work to reduce swelling and pain in the affected areas. The natural compounds present in licorice root, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have been shown to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause inflammation. By reducing the formation of these compounds, herbal licorice capsules can help alleviate symptoms of inflamed gums, promoting healthy gum tissue and a reduction in bleeding and tenderness.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce swelling and combat bacterial infections in the gum tissue, promoting healing and relief from inflammation.

Liquiritigenin: Acts as an antispasmodic, relaxing the muscles around the gumline and reducing pain and discomfort associated with inflamed gums, allowing for easier movement and function of the mouth.
Isoliquiritigenin: Has antioxidant properties, helping to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the gum tissue, which can contribute to inflammation and damage.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in inflamed gums.
Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin has potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help combat infection and inflammation in the gums.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect the gums from oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting healing and reducing pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help alleviate gum inflammation.

Leaves: The leaves are used due to their rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce gum inflammation and promote healing.
Barks: The barks are used because they contain glycosides and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and protect inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 kg of dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra from a trusted supplier or cultivate them in your garden.
Dry the roots further in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder.
Mix 500 mg of the powder with 500 mg of a natural binder like cellulose and 10 mg of silica in a bowl.
Fill the mixture into empty gelatin capsules using a capsule filler machine and seal the capsules tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric capsules has been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the gum tissue, alleviating discomfort and pain associated with gum disease. Additionally, curcumin's antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria that contribute to gingivitis, promoting a healthy oral environment and reducing bleeding and redness. Regular supplementation with herbal turmeric capsules can lead to significant improvement in gum health and overall oral wellness.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums, alleviating pain and discomfort.
Antioxidant activity: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue, reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall oral health.

Cyclooxygenase (COX) inhibition: Curcumin's ability to inhibit COX enzymes reduces the production of pro-inflammatory prostaglandins, which contribute to inflamed gums and other oral inflammatory conditions.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: A polyphenolic compound that helps to reduce inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby alleviating gum inflammation.
Demethoxycurcumin: A curcuminoid that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to prevent the progression of gum inflammation and promote healing.
Tumerone: A sesquiterpene that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce the severity of gum inflammation by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and other pathogens.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric root: Rhyzomes are used to make capsules for inflamed gums because they contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that reduces pain and swelling.
Rhyzomes: Turmeric root extract: Rhyzomes are used to make capsules for inflamed gums because they contain bioactive compounds like curcuminoids that help reduce inflammation and promote healing.

Rhyzomes: Turmeric root powder: Rhyzomes are used to make capsules for inflamed gums because they provide a concentrated source of curcumin and other anti-inflammatory compounds that alleviate gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Grind 500 grams of dried curcuma longa rhizome into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 2 parts of the ground curcuma longa powder with 1 part of gelatin powder in a large bowl.
Add 100 milliliters of boiling water to the mixture and stir until the gelatin is fully dissolved.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 30 minutes then fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dry place for up to 6 months or longer when refrigerated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm capsules helps with inflamed gums because its mucilages, a unique combination of carbohydrates and polysaccharides, form a soothing film that calms the gum tissue. This film protects the gums from irritants and reduces inflammation by shielding them from bacteria, enzymes, and other harmful substances. Additionally, slippery elm's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with inflamed gums, promoting a healthy oral environment and reducing the risk of further complications.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Slippery elm's mucilage helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by gum disease.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The herbs' anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help to reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums.
Antimicrobial activity: Slippery elm's antimicrobial properties can help to combat the growth of bacteria that contribute to gum disease, reducing the severity of inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: These thick, protective compounds help to soothe and coat the inflamed gums, reducing irritation and discomfort.
Tannins: Acting as astringents, tannins help to reduce inflammation, bleeding, and pain in the gums by constricting blood vessels and promoting tissue repair.
Phenolic acids: These antioxidants have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
remedy_parts: Barks: They are used to make capsules due to their astringent properties, which help reduce inflammation and ease gum discomfort.
Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain compounds that help combat bacterial infections and reduce inflammation in the gums.
Roots: The roots of Ulmus rubra are used to make capsules for inflamed gums due to their ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections caused by bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried ulmus rubra bark that has been dried for 2 weeks at 30 degrees celsius.
Grind the dried ulmus rubra bark into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 1:3 ratio of ground ulmus rubra powder to gelatin in a mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with the ulmus rubra powder mixture using a capsule filling machine within 1 hour.
Label and store the herbal ulmus rubra capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with inflamed gums because of their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The capsules contain a unique combination of flavonoids, saponins, and polysaccharides that work synergistically to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial growth in the gums. This natural remedy has been traditionally used to soothe gum pain, reduce swelling, and promote healthy gingivitis prevention. By addressing the root causes of inflamed gums, maidenhair tree capsules provide a safe and effective solution for maintaining oral health and preventing further complications.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Maidenhair tree capsules contain antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gums, which can contribute to gum disease.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and pain in inflamed gums, promoting healing and reducing the severity of symptoms.
Antimicrobial properties: Maidenhair tree contains antimicrobial agents that can help combat bacteria and other microorganisms that contribute to gum inflammation and disease.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids (Quercetin, Kaempferol, and Isorhapontigenin): These flavonoids have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Bilobalide: This triterpene is known for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate inflammation and oxidative stress in the gums, promoting healing and reducing discomfort.

Ginkgolides (Ginkgolide A, B, C, and M): These sesquiterpenes have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help reduce inflammation, prevent tissue damage, and promote the healing of inflamed gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are widely used due to their rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe inflamed gums.
Seeds: The seeds of Ginkgo biloba contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of inflamed gums.
Barks: The barks of Ginkgo biloba contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe inflamed gums and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Dry 500 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves in a single layer at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of the powder with 20 grams of cellulose powder in a bowl.
Use a capsule machine to fill 250 capsules with the powder mixture.
Label and package the capsules in a cool dark place for 24 hours storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with inflamed gums because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds that directly target the source of gum inflammation. The bioactive polyphenols present in elder berries have been shown to reduce prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause pain and swelling in the gums. Additionally, the antioxidants in elder capsules help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to chronic inflammation, promoting a healthy balance in the oral cavity and reducing gum discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Elderberry contains antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the mouth, which can contribute to gum disease and swelling.

remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help reduce inflammation and combat oxidative stress, which are key contributors to inflamed gums.
Phenolic acids (especially ferulic acid): These acids have antimicrobial properties, reducing the bacterial load that can cause gum inflammation and infection.
Saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help soothe and heal inflamed gums by reducing bacterial growth and inflammation.

Leaves: Leaves are used to make capsules to utilize their astringent properties, which help reduce swelling and bleeding in inflamed gums.

remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra berries in late summer to early fall when they are ripe and have turned dark purple color.
Dry the berries in a single layer on paper bags or clean paper at room temperature for 7 to 10 days.
Crush the dried berries into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered berry with a small amount of gelatin or a natural capsule filler to facilitate capsule formation.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powdered berry mixture and seal them using a capsule sealer or a warm water bath.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with inflamed gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby decreasing inflammation. Additionally, ginger's antioxidant properties help protect against damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy gum tissue and reducing inflammation. By targeting both the root causes of inflamed gums, herbal ginger capsules can provide relief from discomfort and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger contains anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which reduce inflammation and pain in the mouth, including inflamed gums.
Antibacterial properties: The phenolic compounds present in ginger exhibit antibacterial activity, helping to combat bacteria that contribute to gum inflammation and disease.
Pain-relieving sesquiterpenes: Ginger's sesquiterpenes, such as bisabolene, have been shown to reduce pain and discomfort associated with inflamed gums, providing relief from symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Ginger rhyzomes contain anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help reduce inflammation and pain in the gums.
Roots: The roots of the ginger plant have been used for centuries to treat oral infections and inflammation, making them a popular choice for medicinal capsules.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 250 grams of dried ginger root and grind it into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered ginger with 500 milliliters of cold water in a saucepan and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes, then strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl.
Add 250 grams of gelatin to the cooled ginger infusion and stir until fully dissolved, allowing it to cool and thicken.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared herbal paste and seal tightly, storing in an airtight container for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow capsules helps with inflamed gums because they contain salicin, a natural compound that closely resembles aspirin. Salicin has potent anti-inflammatory properties, which are able to target the root cause of gum inflammation by reducing swelling and pain. As a result, white willow capsules can provide effective relief from symptoms such as bleeding, redness, and sensitivity, while also promoting healthy gums and preventing further irritation. Additionally, white willow's natural antibacterial properties help combat the bacteria that contribute to gum disease.
remedy_properties: Salicin: Salicin is a key active compound found in white willow bark that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce pain and swelling associated with inflamed gums.
Tannins: Tannins are polyphenolic compounds present in white willow bark that have antibacterial and antiviral properties, which can help combat gum infections and reduce inflammation.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, are found in white willow bark and possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in salicin, a natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory compound, making it effective in reducing gum inflammation.
Barks: Containing salicin and other bioactive compounds, white willow bark is traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of inflamed gums, such as pain and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salix alba leaves from a trusted source ensuring they are free of contaminants.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm ventilated area for 24 hours to remove excess moisture.
Crush the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder processing them into a homogeneous mixture.
Mix 500mg of the powdered salix alba with 100mg of carrier agent such as gelatin or tapioca starch creating a uniform blend.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the blended mixture and seal tightly within one minute of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic capsules helps with inflamed gums because they contain bioactive compounds that possess potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. When taken orally, these compounds are absorbed into the bloodstream and then carried to the affected areas, where they help to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. Additionally, garlic's natural antimicrobial properties can help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to gum inflammation, promoting a healthy mouth and reducing the risk of complications such as tooth loss and chronic pain.
remedy_properties: Allicin: A compound found in garlic that has natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce inflammation and eliminate bacterial infections that can cause gum disease.

Quercetin: A flavonoid antioxidant present in garlic that helps to reduce inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby reducing swelling and pain in inflamed gums.

Methylsulfonylmethane (MSM): A natural sulfur compound found in garlic that has anti-inflammatory properties and can help to reduce swelling and pain in the mouth by decreasing the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sulfur compounds, garlic roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums.
Rhyzomes: The underground stem of garlic, rhyzomes contain allicin, a compound that has antibacterial and antifungal properties, making them effective against gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Measure 500 grams of dried allium sativum powder into a clean glass container for processing.
Mix the powder with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a stainless steel saucepan and reduce heat.
Simmer the mixture for 20 minutes while stirring occasionally to extract active compounds.
Allow the mixture to cool and strain it through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the cooled herbal liquid extract using a capsule filler machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mastich capsules helps with inflamed gums because they contain a blend of natural herbs that have anti-inflammatory properties. The main ingredient, mastiha, has been shown to reduce swelling and pain in the gums by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory compounds. Additionally, other herbs like chamomile and peppermint help soothe and calm the gum tissue, reducing redness and bleeding. By addressing the underlying inflammation, these capsules promote a healthy gum environment and alleviate symptoms of gingivitis and periodontal disease.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Mastiha capsules contain antibacterial compounds that help combat gum-infecting bacteria, reducing inflammation and preventing further infection.
Anti-inflammatory property: The capsules' anti-inflammatory agents help reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums, promoting a faster recovery.
Antioxidant property: Mastiha's antioxidant content helps protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, roots help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in inflamed gums.
Rhyzomes: With their high concentration of essential oils, rhyzomes possess antimicrobial and antifungal activities, which can aid in combating gum infections and promoting healthy oral flora.
remedy_recipe: Gather and dry 500g of fresh pistacia lentiscus leaves ensuring proper identification and handling for quality control.
Crush the dried leaves into small pieces using mortar and pestle to facilitate extraction process.
Combine crushed leaves with 200ml of high-proof ethanol in a ratio of 1:4 and let it steep for 2 weeks stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or filter paper discarding solids and transfer liquid extract to a clean container for concentration.
Fill capsules with 500mg of concentrated liquid extract and seal them immediately for storage and dispensing within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stevia capsules helps with inflamed gums because of its natural antibacterial properties, which reduce the accumulation of plaque and bacteria in the mouth. The herb's anti-inflammatory compounds also soothe and calm irritated gum tissue, reducing swelling and pain. Additionally, stevia has been shown to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, a common culprit behind gum infections. By using herbal stevia capsules, individuals can support healthy gums and maintain good oral health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Stevia's antimicrobial compounds, such as stevioside and rebaudioside, help combat bacteria that contribute to gum inflammation and infection.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Stevia's flavonoids and phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory effects that reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums, promoting healing and soothing irritated tissues.
Antioxidant properties: Stevia's rich antioxidant content helps protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate gum inflammation and disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, stevia roots are effective in reducing gum inflammation and soothing mouth pain.
Barks: Containing a unique blend of flavonoids and terpenes, stevia barks help to combat bacteria that contribute to gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh stevia rebaudiana leaves from a healthy plant.
Dry the leaves in a warm oven at 60°c for 24 hours or until crispy.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 5 grams of the powdered stevia with 95 grams of gelatin and capsule filler to create a blend.
Fill empty capsules with the blend and seal them immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow capsules helps with inflamed gums because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation and soothe swollen tissues. The herb's active compounds, including azulene and flavonoids, have potent anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with gum inflammation. Additionally, yarrow's antiseptic properties help combat bacterial growth, promoting a healthy balance of oral flora and reducing the risk of infection. This natural approach can provide effective relief from inflamed gums without harsh chemicals or pharmaceuticals.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Yarrow capsules contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes that reduce inflammation in the body, including in the gums, by inhibiting prostaglandin synthesis and decreasing the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Antimicrobial properties: The essential oils present in yarrow capsules have antimicrobial effects against various bacteria, viruses, and fungi, which helps to combat gum infections and prevent further inflammation.
Stemming bleeding: Yarrow's hemostatic properties help to constrict blood vessels and stop bleeding, making it useful for treating bleeding gums caused by inflammation or periodontal disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, yarrow roots help soothe swollen and painful gums.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, yarrow flowers have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and fight bacterial infections in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of achillea millefolium at dawn, selecting only those without signs of disease or pest infestation.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer on paper towels or clean cloth for 1-2 hours to reduce moisture content.
Combine dried achillea millefolium leaves with silica gel capsules and 20% glycerin in a ratio of 1:1, then mix well to ensure even distribution.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared mixture, using a small spoon or capsule filler to prevent overfilling, and seal the capsules immediately.
Store the prepared capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for up to a year to maintain potency.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to inflamed gums that are treated with herbal capsules are various types of oral lesions, such as mouth ulcers and canker sores. Herbal remedies like aloe vera, chamomile, and calendula have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and heal the mucous membranes in the mouth. Other conditions, like bad breath (halitosis), tongue thrush, and gum recession, may also be treated with herbal supplements containing ingredients like tea tree oil, garlic, and turmeric.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with inflamed gums is a blend of Triphala, Calendula, and Echinacea. Triphala promotes oral health and reduces inflammation, while Calendula soothes and calms the gums. Echinacea enhances the immune system, fighting off underlying infections that cause inflammation. This synergy of herbs helps to reduce swelling, ease pain, and promote healing in inflamed gums, promoting overall oral well-being and a healthy smile. Regular consumption of these capsules can lead to noticeable improvement in gum health.
